Very often, a rhetorical question draws attention to a negative situation - 7 can't find my coat.' ‘ What's this, then? (= Here it is, stupid.) Who's a lovely baby? (= You’ re a lovely baby.) Do you know what time it is? (= You’ re late.) something. Questions of this kind are called 'rhetorical questions'.

an obvious answer can be used simply as a way of drawing attention to Questions do not always ask for information. In many languages, a question with 1 questions       that    do   not   expect     an   answer 303 rhetorical       questions:      Who     cares?

Where are you going? (     Where you are going?) This word order is not normally possible after a question word.

‘ We're going to Hull  for the weekend.' ‘ You're going to Hull?

In spoken questions, we  do not always put an auxiliary verb before the subject.
